title: Bug in "INSERT INTO ... SELECT" statements that use CASE
key_bug_features: INSERT INTO ... SELECT with CASE statements
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, age INTEGER, salary REAL, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, age, salary) VALUES ('Alice', 30, 60000.00);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, age, salary) SELECT 'Bob', CASE WHEN 25 < 30 THEN 25 ELSE 30 END, NULL;
INSERT INTO test_table (name, age, salary) SELECT 'Charlie', NULL, CASE WHEN age IS NULL THEN 50000.00 ELSE salary END FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
INSERT INTO test_table (name, age, salary) SELECT 'David', 35, CASE WHEN salary IS NOT NULL THEN salary * 1.1 ELSE 70000.00 END FROM test_table WHERE name = 'Alice';
